mnemonics.title: Boot Sequence: FPBKIS
mnemonics.content: Remember 'Firmware, POST, Bootloader, Kernel, Init, Services' for the boot sequence: Firmware (BIOS/UEFI) runs first, POST checks hardware, Bootloader (GRUB) loads kernel, Kernel initializes, Init (PID 1) starts, Services (daemons) launched.

mnemonics.title: BIOS vs UEFI: BIOS is OLD
mnemonics.content: BIOS = Old (text-based, MBR, 2TB limit, no Secure Boot). UEFI = Updated (graphical, GPT, no size limit, Secure Boot, faster). UEFI replaced BIOS in modern computers.

mnemonics.title: MBR Contents: BPS
mnemonics.content: Remember 'BPS' for MBR contents: B = Boot code (446 bytes), P = Partition table (64 bytes, 4 entries), S = Signature (2 bytes, 0x55AA). Total = 512 bytes.
